iti111:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti111:2.1:
“Be consummate in virtue, monks, and consummate in the Pāṭimokkha.
Dwell restrained in accordance with the Pāṭimokkha, consummate in your behavior & sphere of activity.
Train yourselves, having undertaken the training rules, seeing danger in the slightest faults.
iti111:4.2:
then a monk walking with such ardency & compunction is called continually & continuously resolute,
one with persistence aroused.
“If, while he is standing.…
“If, while he is sitting.…
iti111:7.2:
then a monk lying down with such ardency & compunction is called continually & continuously resolute,
one with persistence aroused.”
iti111:8.1: Controlled in walking, controlled in standing,
iti111:8.2: controlled in sitting, controlled in lying down,
iti111:8.3: controlled in flexing & extending his limbs
iti111:9.2: as far as the worlds extend–
iti111:10.1: a monk who dwells thus ardently,
iti111:10.4: training in the mastery of awareness-tranquility–
iti111:10.5: is said to be continually resolute.
